$schema: Design system for docproc — high-level guidelines for AI replication

meta.purpose: Instruct AI and developers to replicate a modern, data-rich dashboard aesthetic. Use this file as the single source of truth for structure, spacing, typography, colors, and component patterns.
meta.aesthetic: Neo-brutalist meets minimalist: raw, utilitarian data presentation softened by rounded corners and a refined palette. Professional, reliable, technologically advanced.

designPrinciples.clarityAndReadability: Prioritize information with strong contrast and typography. Reduce cognitive load.
designPrinciples.visualHierarchy: Differentiate key data, titles, and actions through size, weight, and color. Guide the eye.
designPrinciples.consistency: Uniform cards, buttons, typography, and iconography. Predictable and learnable.
designPrinciples.modularity: UI as distinct, self-contained cards/modules. Flexible and scalable.
designPrinciples.dataFirst: Present data in easily digestible chunks. Charts and metrics are first-class.
designPrinciples.subtleInteractivity: Interactive elements are clear but integrated—accent colors and subtle hover states, no visual noise.
designPrinciples.uncluttered: Generous spacing and minimalist component design despite information density.

structureAndLayout.grid: Flexible grid-based layout. Content arranged in distinct blocks.
structureAndLayout.cardCentric: Primary organizational element is the card. Each card encapsulates one concept or functional group.
structureAndLayout.alignment: Titles and labels left-aligned. Numerical data and metrics right-aligned in lists/tables.
structureAndLayout.responsiveness: Grid adapts: cards stack or reflow. Maintain spacing scale across breakpoints.

spacing.scale: Multiples of 4px or 8px. Use consistently for margins, padding, gaps.
spacing.md: 16px — between elements in a component, label-to-value
spacing.lg: 24px — vertical separation between sections in a card, around headings

typography.fontFamily: Modern, clean sans-serif. Prefer: Inter, Roboto, SF Pro, Open Sans, Plus Jakarta Sans. Prioritize legibility on screen.

weights.bold: 600–700 — card titles, key metrics, table headers, primary CTAs

sizes.sm: 14px — body text, list items, secondary info

typography.monospace: Use for code, addresses, IDs. e.g. Fira Code, SF Mono, Roboto Mono.

cards.border: None by default; rely on background contrast. Optional: theme.borders for subtle outline.
cards.shadow: Light: subtle (see themes.light.shadow). Dark: none or minimal.

buttons.primary: Solid or dark interactive background, primary text color, rounded corners (6px–8px), clear hover state.
buttons.secondary: Transparent or white, border (theme.borders or accent), primary text, rounded corners. e.g. 'See All', 'Stake …'.
buttons.icon: Minimal: icon only, transparent or subtle background. Hover: slight background or accent.

inputs.border: Bottom border or subtle full outline (theme.borders). Focus: accent color border.
inputs.labels: Secondary text color, regular weight, above or left of field.

tablesAndLists.headers: Bold/semi-bold, primary text; optional light background or separator.
tablesAndLists.rows: Consistent vertical spacing. Icons inline where relevant.
tablesAndLists.statusIndicators: Green for positive/up, red for negative/down; small arrows or badges.

chartsAndGraphs.lineCharts: Smooth lines, accent palette. Light grid lines. Clear axis labels (secondary text). Dashed line for 'max limit' (e.g. orange).
chartsAndGraphs.progressBars: Light track, accent fill (e.g. green), rounded ends.
chartsAndGraphs.donutsOrGauges: Segmented circle, percentage in center. Green/red or accent by meaning.

icons.style: Outline or simple solid; consistent weight and language.

badgesAndTags.style: Small rounded rectangles. Light gray or accent-tinted background, primary or contrast text.

links.style: Accent color (e.g. blue). Underline optional. Clear hover state.

pagination.style: Text 'PAGE X OF Y' plus < and > controls. Buttons match secondary button style.

codeSnippets.background: Slightly different from card or subtle border.

avatarsAndMedia.style: Square or circular, rounded corners, optional subtle border. Contained in card layout.

usageNotesForAI: Prefer theme tokens (e.g. theme.backgrounds.card) over hardcoded hex in narrative; in code use CSS variables or design tokens derived from this file.
Cards are the main building block: group every major section in a card with consistent padding and radius.
Use the spacing scale (xs–xl) for all margins and padding; avoid arbitrary values.
Reserve large font sizes for hero numbers and key metrics; keep body and UI at sm/base.
In data-heavy views, right-align numbers and use green/red only for deltas or status.
Keep charts simple: distinct accent colors, light grids, clear labels.
Buttons: primary for one main action per context; secondary/ghost for the rest.
Maintain one accent (e.g. blue) for links and primary actions; use semantic colors (green/red) for status and trends.
Dark theme: avoid heavy shadows; use elevation via background contrast (primary vs card vs interactive).
Light theme: use subtle shadows on cards to lift them from the background.
